steps.title: Apply the charge threshold
steps.content: The text says wintering honeybees have a maximum positive charge high enough to facilitate electrostatic pollen transfer. A pollinator with an even greater maximum positive charge would be expected to show the same capacity.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice A says species with greater charges should not show the capacity, contradicting the threshold logic. Choice B is vague and unsupported. Choice C claims the minimum necessary charge must exceed 300 pC, which the data do not establish. The answer is D.
